title: crataegus monogyna subsp. kyrtostyla
intro: Crataegus monogyna subsp. kyrtostyla, also known as the common hawthorn, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardio-protective effects, making it a potential treatment for high blood pressure, cholesterol, and cardiovascular diseases. The health benefits of Crataegus monogyna subsp. kyrtostyla include improving heart health, reducing inflammation, and lowering cholesterol levels, as well as exhibiting antiviral and antibacterial properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or extract, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional medicine and herbal supplements.
